headlines.title: Man who was on hantavirus cruise ship says Nebraska isolation center feels like ‘prison’
headlines.description: A man from New York State who was on board the Hondius cruise ship — which became the center of an outbreak of hantavirus that killed three people earlier this month — compared being forced to quarantine at the Nebraska health facility to being in “prison.”The 30-year-old man, who asked not to be identified because of concerns for his safety and harassment of other named passengers, told NBC News by phone that he wants to be able to quarantine at home.

headlines.title: Absence of USAID likely slowed Ebola detection and response, former officials say
headlines.description: The Trump administration hollowed out USAID last year, but the State Department denied the changes have hampered its response.

headlines.title: From Omaha Steaks to hazmat suits: What life was like for contagion patients in quarantine
headlines.description: As more than 40 Americans remain in quarantine for up to six weeks following a hantavirus outbreak aboard a cruise ship, former patients who spent time inside some of the country’s highest-security medical isolation units during previous viral contagions are sharing what it’s like to endure weeks cut off from the outside world.

headlines.title: Good news: You don’t need surgery. But you’ll have to apply maggots to your wound.
headlines.description: The lowly maggot gets a bad rap, mostly known for feeding on corpses and rotting meat.

headlines.title: Life Lessons for Recent Grads, Anyone Seeking Career Change
headlines.description: Whether you are a recent graduate or someone simply looking for a change, Jodi Kantor, author of the book, “How to Start: Discovering Your Life’s Work” joins TODAY to share life lessons about carving out a fulfilling career. She breaks down how to turn passion into a job by pairing a craft with a need, shares how to combat the existential threat of AI, and gives advice for managing parents' expectations with individual dreams.

headlines.title: David Letterman Talks Racing Passion, Heart Scare, Late Night TV
headlines.description: TODAY’s Craig Melvin met up with comedy legend David Letterman at Indianapolis Motor Speedway, the home of Letterman’s Indy race team. After leaving “The Late Show,” Letterman teamed up with businessman Mike Lanigan and former Indy driver Bobby Rahal, all three of whom had serious heart scares, to co-own a race team dedicated to raising awareness about the dangers of heart valve failure. Letterman gives an update on his health and praises today’s hosts of late night comedy.

headlines.title: Get the Most out of Your Annual Physical With These Tips
headlines.description: NBC News medical contributor Dr. Kavita Patel joins TODAY to share tips for getting the most out of your annual physical, including preparing your medical history ahead of time, writing down questions, taking notes or even recording the visit. She also advises patients to be open and honest with their doctors.

headlines.title: Surgeon General Issues Suggested Screen Time Limits for Kids
headlines.description: There is a new warning about screen time coming from the surgeon general’s office, raising the alarm on the amount of time kids spend on screens, the potential harms of screen use, problematic content they're consuming, and more. NBC’s Kate Snow joins TODAY with details from the report, including some of the surgeon general's recommendations.

headlines.title: Is the Forme Power Bra really worth $179?
headlines.description: I tested the Forme Power Bra for five months to see if it could improve my posture and back support. Here’s what actually happened.

headlines.title: The best running watches, tested over hundreds of miles
headlines.description: I’ve tested dozens of running watches from Garmin, Coros, Amazfit, Fitbit, Apple and more. Here are my top picks for beginners, races and ultra-athletes alike.

headlines.title: The best men’s walking shoes of 2026, tested and reviewed
headlines.description: Shop expert-recommended and staff-tested men’s walking shoes from Brooks, On, New Balance and Hoka.

headlines.title: I’ve tested dozens of workout headphones: These are the best
headlines.description: I tested over a dozen workout headphones from brands like Apple, Beats, and Shokz to find our top picks for running, biking, the gym and more.
